title: Matisia jefensis
intro:
There is not enough information available about Matisia jefensis. However, I can provide general information about the plant family Matisia, which belongs to the family Malvaceae and is native to the Americas.

The Matisia genus is a group of flowering plants that includes several species of trees and shrubs, commonly known as "cancha" or "tola". Matisia species have been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Andean region. The health benefits of Matisia species include their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. 

The bioactive constituents of Matisia species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Matisia species are infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections, wounds, and respiratory issues.
